beats.text: This area in the Persian Gulf was quiet through the earlier hours of the day.
At 10:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 97% jamming probability for 22:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
22:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in the Persian Gulf was quiet through the earlier hours of the day.
At 10:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 95% jamming probability for 22:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
22:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
This area in the monitored area was quiet through the earlier hours of the day.
At 00:00 UTC the system committed a public forecast: 86% jamming probability for 12:00 UTC — 12 hours ahead.
12:00 UTC arrived: GNSS integrity degraded exactly here. Verified hit — called 12 hours in advance, provably committed before the event.
The record keeps its failures too. This point sits in the eastern Baltic.
At 00:00 UTC the interference channel lit up here. The 12-hours-ahead forecast had put the risk at just 1%.
Interference appeared here without a high-risk flag. It stays on the record — misses are published, never hidden.
